text.title: Asymmetric Churn Power Interview Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Asymmetric Churn Power Interview Protocol plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The protocol MUST define the churn cohort first (segment + time window + minimum usage threshold) before any interview is scheduled; ad-hoc cohorts MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc churn cohorts mix signal (real PMF gaps) with noise (trial dropouts); the upfront definition is the structural sample frame.

rule.statement: For every 3 churn interviews, the protocol MUST also conduct 1 power-user interview; churn-only protocols MUST be reclassified.
rule.rationale: Churn-only research describes the negative space; the asymmetric ratio (3:1) keeps the positive signal as comparator.

rule.statement: Interview questions MUST be open-ended (no yes/no, no leading phrases like 'would you pay for…'); leading questions MUST be rewritten.
rule.rationale: Leading questions produce confirmation; open questions surface unanticipated reasons.

rule.statement: Every transcript MUST be coded by ≥2 independent coders with inter-coder agreement ≥0.7 (Cohen's kappa); single-coder protocols MUST be rejected.

rationale.source: Saldaña — The Coding Manual for Qualitative Researchers

rule.rationale: Single-coder tagging projects the coder's hypothesis; dual coding with kappa is the structural reliability gate.

rule.statement: Every reported theme MUST name the number of interviews it appeared in (e.g., 7/12); themes without evidence counts MUST be returned for revision.
rule.rationale: Theme-without-count obscures whether 'users want X' came from 1 or 11 interviews; counts make claims verifiable.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
